structure.notes: The note preserves the Stage 0 skeleton, has the required sections, labels assumptions, and correctly treats the locked tuple as a PartialID anchor despite the inherited Regression estimator section title. The §14 checklist is usable and lists the main observable, latent, probabilistic, deterministic, convex-analysis, and counterexample objects.

novelty.notes: The derivation is at most subfield: the positive result is a standard finite latent-response LP sharp-set/projection statement plus monotonicity under dropping equations, and the negative result refutes the Stage -1 product formula by a compatibility counterexample. That is useful, but below novelty_target=field because it does not prove a regime-opening PartialID theorem or a new strictly tighter analytic bound beyond generic Balke-Pearl/Autobounds-style machinery.

correctness.notes: The coupled-memory LP and product-envelope counterexample arithmetic are mostly plausible, but the coarsened-law definition and strict memory-gain witness are not sufficiently correct/reviewable when e2 depends on the discarded Z1 label. The proof also treats LP sharpness largely by definition rather than fully proving equivalence between the mean LP and the maintained causal model.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem \ref{thm:sharp-memory}
perItemFindings.one_line: The equality Θ_I(P)=[inf,sup] is essentially definitional once Θ_I is defined as the projection of L_mem, and the coarsening containment is standard feasible-set monotonicity.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem \ref{thm:sharp-memory}, proof lines 388-391
perItemFindings.one_line: The coarsened feasible set L_coarse(AP,e) is not formally defined enough to justify reusing the same latent completion when e2 is allowed to depend on the discarded Z1 label.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem \ref{thm:product-fails} and counterexample lines 442-462
perItemFindings.one_line: The product-envelope strictness arithmetic checks for the memory-retaining cell: J_N=[1/10,3/5], J_M=[1/4,3/4], the product allows u1=1, and the coupled complier upper endpoint is 9/10.

perItemFindings.label: Proposition \ref{prop:strict-aggregation}, counterexample lines 464-464
perItemFindings.one_line: The claimed coarsened interval containing [0,1] is not established: after summing out Z1 while e2(1|x,0,0)=1/3 and e2(1|x,1,0)=2/3, the composition among Z2=1 cells changes, so the stated collapsed complier share 1/3 and responder outcome contribution 1/5 do not follow from the observable coarsened equations.

perItemFindings.label: Sanity check: MTR specialization lines 421-421
perItemFindings.one_line: Adding unit/type-level MTR is stronger than merely intersecting the final contrast interval with [0,1]; the note should either weaken this to a mean-level shape restriction or prove the LP projection really reduces to intersection.

perItemFindings.label: Related work and positioning lines 83-91
perItemFindings.one_line: The literature review is targeted, but it acknowledges Autobounds/generic discrete sharp-bound machinery as the closest comparator and does not isolate a field-level mathematical advance beyond a hand-indexed specialization and counterexample.

verbatim_critique: Structure is acceptable for Stage 0: all required roles are present, the locked parameter block at lines 150-172 identifies a PartialID anchor, assumptions are labelled, and the Formalization checklist at lines 466-479 is actionable at the math-object level. The main defects are novelty and correctness. On novelty, the actually derived kernel is below the orchestrator floor: tier=subfield below novelty_target=field. The sharp-memory theorem mostly says that the identified set is the projection of the finite latent-response LP and that deleting equations cannot tighten the identified set. That is the standard response-type/LP partial-identification construction familiar from Balke-Pearl-style bounds and generic discrete sharp-bound systems such as Autobounds. The product-subtraction failure is a useful correction to the Stage -1 conjecture, but it is a compatibility counterexample within this standard machinery, not a regime-opening new PartialID result. A field-tier repair would need a genuinely new analytic reduction, compatibility characterization, or sharp bound formula that generic LP projection does not already supply. On correctness, the memory-retaining product-failure arithmetic in Section Counterexample or minimality check is mostly sound: J_N={0}×[1/10,3/5], J_M={0}×[1/4,3/4], the product construction allows u1=1, and the coupled complier projection has upper endpoint 2/5+1/2=9/10. However, the strict memory-gain part is not established. The note explicitly allows e2 to depend on Z1 and chooses e2(1|x,0,0)=1/3 and e2(1|x,1,0)=2/3. Once Z1 is coarsened away inside D1=0, the composition of the Z2=1 observed cells is not the same as the unconditional D1=0 composition: compliers enter only through the Z1=0 branch, while never-takers enter through both branches with different Z2 probabilities. The line-464 statement that the collapsed D1=0 branch has complier share 1/3, response rate 1/2, and observed responder outcome contribution 1/5 ignores this assignment-induced reweighting and is insufficient to prove the displayed completions reproduce AP. This also exposes a definition gap in L_coarse(AP,e): if Z1 is discarded but the known second-stage law depends on Z1, the coarsened assignment law and the coarsened sequential-randomization restriction must be specified precisely. The proof of Theorem \ref{thm:sharp-memory} at lines 388-391 cannot rely on simply adding equations unless the coarsened feasible set is exactly the image relaxation induced by those summed equations. Finally, the LP sharpness claim should be proved rather than asserted: lines 140 and 311 say the mean LP is sharp, but the proof only establishes linearity/compactness and does not construct, from arbitrary feasible (a,b), bounded potential-outcome kernels or a latent causal completion reproducing the relevant observed first moments under all stated restrictions. These issues are fixable, but the current note should not pass Stage 0.5 under a field novelty floor.
